goal: Raise the shared Orbit visual system from a functional workbench to a compact participant-facing product surface by tightening color, type, spacing, focus, button, chip, panel, and specimen treatments while preserving existing route behavior.

success_criteria.description: shared/ui/theme.ts exports named semantic tokens for canvas, surface, text, muted text, border, primary action, evidence, confirmation, privacy, warning, and success; app/globals.css mirrors those tokens as --orbit-* variables, uses letter-spacing: 0, and avoids viewport-width font scaling.

app/globals.css defines one visual system for panels, chips, buttons, fields, focus-visible, details disclosures, and dense grids using 8px-or-less card radius, stable min/max dimensions for controls, and no decorative orb, bokeh, or dominant purple-gradient background.

shared/ui/primitives.tsx exposes reusable product primitives for frame, surface, chip, field, primary action, secondary action, token swatch, and inline metric or status display; rendered primitives contain no inline style attributes and keep unknown chip tones on the neutral tone.

GET /dev/foundation/style returns a representative product specimen using the shared primitives for relationship context, source evidence, confirmation guard, tokens, controls, chips, and action states; desktop, tablet, and 375px mobile evidence shows no overlapping text or horizontal overflow.

tests/ui/theme.test.ts asserts the visual tokens, CSS guardrails, primitive rendering, specimen route content, non-nested card pattern, and representative contrast ratios; npm test and npm run build pass.

out_of_scope: Adding or changing backend capability behavior
Changing API envelopes or service contracts
Rewriting route-specific product copy for /app/profile, /app/contacts, /app/events, /app/followups, /app/chat, /app/dashboard, or /app/agent
Introducing auth, session, persistence, provider, or live data integration
Editing feature folders or tests outside the declared visual-system boundary
